tables.description: Dataset provides results of neurological testing, e.g. muscle strength of extremities, reflexes, coordination, somatosensory function of extremities (including pain).
Dataset provides motor functionality data of fingers, arms, trunk, ocular movements, degree of dystonia, chorea, and total motor score.

Dataset provides data of participants' psychological status, including feelings of depression, apathy, low self-esteem, compulsive behavior, delusions, confusion, dementia, and queries use of pharmacotherapy.
Inclusion/Exclusion Exam dataset describes study inclusion/exclusion criteria (e.g. HD symptoms, other illnesses, medications taken, ability to reliably take medication).

variables.name: ELIGIBIL
variables.description: 30 Is the Subject eligible for enrollment?

variables.name: INEX1
variables.description: 1. Does the subject have clinical features of HD and a confirmatory family history of HD, or a CAG repeat expansion >= 36?

variables.name: INEX10
variables.description: 10. If subject is female of child bearing potential, does she meet protocol specific eligibility criteria?

variables.name: INEX11
variables.description: 11. Does the subject have a history of known sensitivity or intolerability to CoQ10?

variables.name: INEX12
variables.description: 12. Has the subject been exposed to any investigational drug within 30 days of the Baseline Visit?

variables.name: INEX13
variables.description: 13. In the Investigator's judgment, does the subject have any clinical evidence of unstable medical illness?

variables.name: INEX14
variables.description: 14. In the Investigator's judgment, does the subject have any clinical evidence of unstable psychiatric illness, defined as psychosis (hallucinations or delusions) or untreated major depression or suicidal ideation within 90 days of the Baseline Visit?

variables.name: INEX15
variables.description: 15. Does the subject have a history of alcohol or drug abuse within 1 year of the Baseline Visit?

variables.name: INEX16
variables.description: 16. If female, is the subject currently pregnant or lactating?

variables.name: INEX17
variables.description: 17. Has the subject used supplemental CoQ10 within 120 days prior to Baseline Visit or (after Amendment 5 approval) within 30 days?

variables.name: INEX18B
variables.description: 18b. Was the alanine aminotransferase (ALT) value > three times the upper limit of normal?

variables.name: INEX18C
variables.description: 18c. Was the total bilirubin value > three times the upper limit of normal?

variables.name: INEX18F
variables.description: 18f. Was the hematocrit level < 33 for female and < 35 for male?

variables.name: INEX18G
variables.description: 18g. Was the coagulation test > 1.5 times the upper limit of normal?

variables.name: INEX19
variables.description: 19. Does the subject have a known allergy to color additive FD&C #5 (also known as tartrazine) or any other ingredient in the study drug?

variables.name: INEX2
variables.description: 2. Does the subject have a TFC score >= 9?

variables.name: INEX3
variables.description: 3. Is the subject independently ambulatory and not requiring skilled nursing care?

variables.name: INEX4
variables.description: 4. Will the subject be 16 years of age or older at the Baseline Visit?

variables.name: INEX5
variables.description: 5. Does the subject meet either of these criteria (if either a or b is true, enter 1 = Yes):  a. Has not taken psychotropic medication (e.g. anxiolytics, hypnotics, benzodiazepines, antidepressants) within the 60 days prior to the baseline visit, or  b. If the subject has taken psychotropic medications within 60 days prior to the baseline visit, the dose has been stable for four weeks prior to randomization and is expected to be maintained at a constant dose throughout the study.

variables.name: INEX6
variables.description: 6. Is the subject able to take oral medication?

variables.name: INEX7
variables.description: 7. Is there an informant/caregiver who is willing and able to supervise the daily dosage of all study medication to the subject and maintain control of study medication in the home, or (after Amendment 5 approval) has the investigator determined that this subject is independently highly functioning and can enter the study without a caregiver?

variables.name: INEX8
variables.description: 8. Is the subject willing and able to comply with study requirements and give informed consent?

variables.name: INEX9
variables.description: 9. Is there a designated individual identified to participate in the ongoing consent process should the subject become compromised during the study?

variables.name: consent_day
variables.description: A. day informed consent was signed

tables.description: Dataset describes adverse event incidents and their results (dose reduction, drug discontinuation, study withdrawal, etc.).

variables.name: REVPWD_day
variables.description: 44 Day of revocation of Premature Withdrawal

variables.name: RTNTODRG
variables.description: 46 Is subject returning to study drug

variables.name: SAEREL
variables.description: 9 Serious adverse event relationship to study drug

tables.description: Dataset provides data of participants' daily functioning, including ability to take care of oneself, cook, walk, manage finances, etc.

variables.name: FAINOTBD
variables.description: 65. Get in and out of bed

tables.description: Adverse Events dataset describes the type of adverse events (how the subject was affected, e.g. psychologically/physically), time frame, actions taken and outcome of the adverse events.
Dataset describes absence/presence of HD history in parents, and alcohol and tobacco use of participants.

variables.name: MNTLP_YRS
variables.description: 15 Mental health help year from randomization

tables.description: Dataset describes symptoms at cranial nerves.
Autopsy reports results obtained from different brain areas (e.g. basal ganglia, cortex, n. accumbens), and latency from death to autopsy.
Concomitant Medication provides information about medication taken, name, dosage, administration rout, etc.

variables.name: WHODRUG
variables.description: 18 WHO WHO (World Health Organization) DRUG NAME

tables.description: University of Rochester Medical Center laboratory data  include detailed results of blood and urine testing, e.g. bilirubin, creatinine, red/white blood cells, platelet counts, glucose, yeast, protein, uric acid.

variables.description: SI Result (International standard of units lab result)

tables.description: The dataset includes laboratory test results for creatinine, coagulation profile, liver function etc. from the Australian site.

variables.description: 13 SI Result - International standard of units lab result (numeric)

variables.description: 14 SI Unit - International standard of units lab test units

tables.description: Dataset provides socio-demographic information e.g. gender, education, ethnicity, but also "handedness".
Dataset provides information about circumstances of death, time frame, possible cause.
Dataset describes Mayo clinic laboratory test results (blood -, coagulation panel -, urine analyses, etc.).
Dataset describes blood, urine and DNA sample collection results.

variables.description: 4 Blood for safety labs Collection Day

tables.description: Dataset provides information about participants' current tobacco use (e.g. cigars, cigarettes, smokeless tobacco).

tables.description: Dataset provides data obtained at the screening visit, including socio-demographic data and referral source.

variables.description: Identify self as Hawaiian/Other Pacif Is

tables.description: The study conclusion dataset provides information about time frame of study conclusion (following enrollment), reason for early withdrawal if appropriate.

variables.description: 3 Did the subject complete the study?

variables.name: FollowConsent_day
variables.description: 7 Informed consent day for follow up

variables.description: Withdrawal Reason at time study was stopped

tables.description: Dataset provides information about deaths that occurred during treatment (based on Incident, Conclusion, AE and Mortality data tables), cause, if HD related or not, prior Total Functioning Capacity score when available.
Dataset provides data of cognitive functioning, including reading, color naming, symbol - digit modality testing results.

variables.name: CASYDIGX
variables.description: 5 Symbol Digit Modalities Test - not done

tables.description: Dataset describes basic physiological/anthropometric measurements (e.g. blood pressure, temperature, height, weight).
Adverse Effect Follow Up (after study exit) describes the type of adverse effect, time frame (from day of randomization) and resolution.

variables.name: FOLLOWBY
variables.description: 3 AE (Adverse Effect) to be followed by

tables.description: Dataset provides summary scores for participants' ability to manage finances, domestic chores, activities of daily living, type of occupation participant is able to hold, and total functioning score.
The dataset reports query results used to determine if treatment could be guessed by subject or investigator.
Dataset describes general medical history of participant during childhood (preterm birth, childhood diseases, head- other injuries, etc.).

variables.name: MHHX
variables.description: 3 Category History - Any history of MHTERM

variables.name: MHROW
variables.description: 2 Category Seq. Num. (The form allowed for three rows or events within each category.  MHROW counts the 3 rows.)

tables.description: Dataset 2 of medical history - description of HD symptoms (i.e. presence/absence of HD - motor/cognitive/psychiatric/other - symptoms), time frame of data collection and who noticed/reports symptoms.
Dataset describes body areas/systems with HD symptoms (e.g. auditory, gastrointestinal, visual).

variables.name: MHDIAG_YR
variables.description: 6 Year of Diagnosis - in years from enrollment

tables.description: Dataset provides information about difference between previous and recent testing results, and confidence level of HD diagnosis.
Dataset describes CoE Q10 return (unused or empty/used)
Dataset provides basic information about health exams (e.g. cardiovascular/ENT/neurological) performed.

variables.name: PESEQ
variables.description: 1 Number - sequence or row numbers from the physical exam form

tables.description: Dataset 3 of medical history - description of HD symptoms (predicted/confirmed), genetic testing performed/not.
Dataset describes the dosage of coenzyme Q10 (chewable wafers) taken.
Dataset describes CoE Q10 dosage dispersal.
Dataset describes results of pregnancy testing.
The dataset provides information about the coenzyme Q10 study dosage taken.
DNA consent - information about permission to retain DNA.
Dataset provides information about treatment group assignment.
Dataset provides information about institutionalization of participants and ability to continue (or not) study participation.
CAG reports CAG repeat length determined.
Dataset 1 of medical history - diagnoses collection (incl. presence/absence of HD symptoms), time frame of data collection.
Dataset provides description of HD symptoms (e.g. no symptoms, no significant disability - severe disability).
Dataset provides information about the extent of assistance required with physical care (Independence Scale).
Dataset describes the dosage of coenzyme Q10 taken per study day.

variables.description: Current dose (number of wafers per day)
